fields.description:
## Description
Re-point the code_health metric specs at the analyzer + snapshot store instead of scanning `src/rebar/**/*.py` and `.github/module-size-limit.txt`, so a non-Python client gets honest Unavailable rather than fabricated zeros. Does NOT touch the CI module-size GATE (separate governance). No LLM/prompt/schema surface is introduced (T8 not-applicable); the only data shapes are the existing `plan_review`-independent metric dicts and the snapshot NDJSON record.

## Mechanism, per metric (G6 — mechanism correctness)
- `module_size_distribution`, `oversized_module_count`: compute from `AnalyzerResult.loc` + config `size_cap`/`size_near_fraction`. `oversized_module_count` is today a seed stub (`grep -n "_no_data_yet" src/rebar/metrics/registry.py` confirms) -> gains a real compute here.
- `module_size_trend`: reads the TIME SERIES from the existing snapshot store via `rebar.metrics.snapshot.read_snapshots(since, until)`; input = stored records `{ts, record:{max_loc, over_cap_count}}`; output = ordered `[{ts, value}]`; `Unavailable` when zero snapshots in range. Rationale: trend REQUIRES history, and the NDJSON snapshot store is the only historical source already present (`test -f src/rebar/metrics/snapshot.py`), so no new persistence is invented.
- `cap_change_events`: tracks edits to `.github/module-size-limit.txt`, a rebar-governance artifact with NO client analogue. DECISION: DEREGISTER the spec from the portable registry (remove from `register()`), documented in the ticket-close note. Rationale: keeping a rebar-path-specific metric contradicts the epics portability goal; it can live as a rebar-only opt-in later. Deregistration is the minimal correct mechanism (vs. leaving a metric that fabricates/raises on clients).

## Grounding (E4/G1G2 — verified)
- CONFIRMED `grep -n "module-size-limit\\|src.*rebar.*rglob\\|_read_cap" src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py`: the hardcodes live at :35 (`_LIMIT_FILE`), `_read_cap` :75, scan root `src/rebar` :93, and `cap_change_events` :167 registered ~:272.
- Existing tests pin the OLD behavior and MUST be updated: `tests/unit/test_git_metrics.py`, `tests/unit/test_git_metrics_heldout.py`, `tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py`. `analyzer.py` is from 3b30 (depends_on) — consumed, not created.

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] No metric reader references the old hardcodes. PROVE: `! grep -Eq "src/rebar|module-size-limit.txt" src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`.
- [ ] `cap_change_events` no longer registered. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py::test_cap_change_events_deregistered` (asserts the id absent from the rendered registry).
- [ ] Unconfigured analyzer -> Unavailable, NOT `{count:0,max_loc:0}` at confidence=high, NO leaked errno. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py::test_foreign_repo_honest_unavailable`.
- [ ] `module_size_trend` returns an ordered series from snapshots (Unavailable when none). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_module_size_trend.py`.
- [ ] git-delta + agent_process metrics unchanged; updated git_metrics tests green. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/test_git_metrics.py tests/unit/test_git_metrics_heldout.py`.

## Dependencies: 3b30, 18f9, 21fb, 3ba0 (recorded depends_on edges).
## Files: src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py, src/rebar/metrics/registry.py, tests/unit/test_git_metrics.py, tests/unit/test_git_metrics_heldout.py, tests/interfaces/test_metrics_cli.py, tests/unit/metrics/test_module_size_trend.py (new)
## Scope: M-L

## Plan corrections (round 4 — supersede any conflicting text above)
- TEST FILE PATHS (E4/G1G2): the files to update are `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py` and `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics_heldout.py` (the `metrics/` subdirectory), NOT `tests/unit/test_git_metrics*.py`.
- SIZE CAP SOURCE (G1G2/G6): `size_cap` and `size_near_fraction` come from the `[code_health]` config section created by prerequisite `[rebar:e7a0-acef-ec67-42bb]`, resolved onto the context object; the compute path reads them from `ctx` (no hardcoded file). module_size_distribution / oversized_module_count compute from `AnalyzerResult [rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]`.loc plus that cap.
- POSITIVE-PATH AC (E6/F1): add — [ ] with a configured analyzer, module_size_distribution / oversized_module_count return CORRECT values. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_configured_positive` feeding a synthetic `AnalyzerResult [rebar:3b30-2168-ba16-41fc]` with known LOC (files at 100/850, cap 800 -> over_cap_count==1, max_loc==850).
- module_size_trend DESCOPED (G6/E4/T1 writer-gap): no snapshot WRITER produces `{max_loc, over_cap_count}` records yet, so module_size_trend stays a seed stub returning Unavailable in THIS ticket; wiring it to a real time series needs a scc-based snapshot writer, which is OUT OF SCOPE (deferred to a follow-up). The earlier claim that it reads existing snapshot records is withdrawn. Revised AC: — [ ] module_size_trend returns Unavailable (no writer yet). PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py::test_trend_unavailable`.
